claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 is divisible by 8.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the Euler totient phi(n^2 - 1) is divisible by 8.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the divisor count tau(n^2 - 1) is at least 8.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the product phi(n^2 - 1) * tau(n^2 - 1) is divisible by 64.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then n^2 is congruent to 1 or 19 modulo 30.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then n^2 is congruent to 1 or 49 modulo 120.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then (n^2 - 1) * (n^2 - 49) is divisible by 960.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the 2-adic valuation vp(n^2 - 1, 2) is at least 3.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the 3-adic valuation vp(n^2 - 1, 3) is at least 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the sum of valuations vp(n^2 - 1, 2) + vp(n^2 - 1, 3) is at least 4.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 2^vp(n^2 - 1, 2) * 3^vp(n^2 - 1, 3) is a multiple of 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the greatest common divisor gcd(n^2 - 1, 24) is exactly 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^4 - 1 is divisible by 240.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then gcd(n^2 - 1, n^4 - 1) is a multiple of 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then the Euler totient phi(n^4 - 1) is divisible by 64.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then gcd(phi(n^2 - 1), phi(n^4 - 1)) is a multiple of 8.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 is a multiple of 12.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then (n^2 - 1)/4 is an even integer.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then (n^2 - 1)/3 is an even integer.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then (n^2 - 1)/12 is an even integer.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^6 - 1 is divisible by 504.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then n^12 - 1 is divisible by 5040.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then tau(n^12 - 1) is at least 60.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then phi(n^12 - 1) is a multiple of phi(5040).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 + 23 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 + 23) is at least 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 + 23) is exactly 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 + 47 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 + 47) is a multiple of 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the triple gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 + 23, n^2 + 47) is exactly 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then sigma(n) is an even integer.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the product sigma(n) * ((n^2 - 1)/12) is even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then phi(n) is even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then phi(n) * sigma(n) is divisible by 8.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then phi(n) * sigma(n) is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then n^2 - 25 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 - 25) is a multiple of 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 - 25) is exactly 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then n^2 - 49 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then the triple gcd(n^2 - 1, n^2 - 25, n^2 - 49) is exactly 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^3 - n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^5 - n is divisible by 120.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then gcd(n^3 - n, n^5 - n) is a multiple of 24.
predicate: (not (is_prime(n) and n > 5)) or (gcd(n**3 - n, n**5 - n) % 24 == 0)

claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then gcd(n^3 - n, n^5 - n) is exactly equal to n^3 - n.
predicate: (not (is_prime(n) and n > 5)) or (gcd(n**3 - n, n**5 - n) == n**3 - n)

claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 5 then n^7 - n is divisible by 504.
